messages.content:
Return an id only when exactly one record satisfies all requirements. If none or more than one qualifies, abstain with INSUFFICIENT.
Records:
  - jade: mass 1, glow 2, span 2, charge 5
  - fawn: mass 5, glow 1, span 3, charge 9
  - rust: mass 1, glow 8, span 3, charge 1
  - ochre: mass 8, glow 3, span 8, charge 1
  - pearl: mass 5, glow 9, span 3, charge 7
  - lilac: mass 3, glow 1, span 2, charge 6
  - amber: mass 1, glow 7, span 7, charge 5
  - indigo: mass 7, glow 6, span 6, charge 6
Requirements: charge <= 2 and span >= 4 and glow <= 2.
End with exactly one line:
ANSWER: <answer>

Audit this worked procedure. It starts at mut-nak-tur-net-lax, contains exactly one first error, and later lines continue from the erroneous state. Locate the first error, recompute from there, and return the correct final state.
  step 1 (if the first item is mun or tur, rotate left once; otherwise rotate right once): mut-nak-tur-net-lax -> lax-mut-nak-tur-net
  step 2 (advance every item 5 step(s) in the cycle): lax-mut-nak-tur-net -> mun-lax-net-bom-mut
  step 3 (if the first item is mun or tur, rotate left once; otherwise rotate right once): mun-lax-net-bom-mut -> lax-bom-bom-mut-mun
  step 4 (reverse the entire sequence): lax-bom-bom-mut-mun -> mun-mut-bom-bom-lax
Cycle order: mun nak lax bom mut tur net (after the last item, wrap to the first).
End with exactly one line:
ANSWER: <answer>

A worker starts with P · H · R · J · P · H · M and applies: reverse the entire sequence; then advance every item 6 step(s) in the cycle. They claim the result is R · M · A · H · J · M · A. Verify the claim.
Cycle order: A J P R W M D H (after the last item, wrap to the first).
End with exactly one line: ANSWER: YES or ANSWER: NO

Audit this worked procedure. It starts at F-K-K-F-D-H, contains exactly one first error, and later lines continue from the erroneous state. Locate the first error, recompute from there, and return the correct final state.
  step 1 (advance every item 2 step(s) in the cycle): F-K-K-F-D-H -> M-H-H-M-K-R
  step 2 (if the first item is J or F, rotate left once; otherwise rotate right once): M-H-H-M-K-R -> R-M-H-H-M-K
  step 3 (swap positions 1 and 3): R-M-H-H-M-K -> H-M-R-H-M-K
  step 4 (if the first item is F or C, rotate left once; otherwise rotate right once): H-M-R-H-M-K -> K-H-M-R-F-M
Cycle order: J D F K M H C R (after the last item, wrap to the first).
End with exactly one line:
ANSWER: <answer>

Choose the single probe that best distinguishes these three candidate rules. Score a probe by how many distinct outputs the hypotheses predict; choose the unique maximum.
Hypotheses:
  H1: if the first item is bin or bar, rotate left once; otherwise rotate right once
  H2: overwrite position 4 with tur
  H3: if the first item is ron or bar, rotate left once; otherwise rotate right once
Candidate probes:
  P1: kek · bin · ron · ron
  P2: ron · sim · bar · ron
  P3: tur · bar · sim · kek
Cycle order: tur ron nuk kek sim bin bar (after the last item, wrap to the first).
End with exactly one line: ANSWER: P1, ANSWER: P2, or ANSWER: P3

A worker starts with lem-cor-gim-nix-jor and applies: reverse the entire sequence; then advance every item 5 step(s) in the cycle. They claim the result is nix-jor-fal-ruz-kav. Verify the claim.
